requirements.text: WHEN a rendered Wireloom block is displayed, the system shall show a visible annotation mode toggle control on the block.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Functional - A rendered Wireloom block offers a visible control

requirements.text: WHEN user selects full callout mode, the system shall display Wireloom annotations as always-visible callout boxes (current default behavior).
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Full callout mode preserves current readable annotation view

requirements.text: WHEN user selects compact mode, the system shall replace always-visible callout boxes with numbered target markers at the same positions.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Compact mode replaces always-visible callout boxes with numbered target markers

requirements.text: WHEN user hovers over a compact marker, the system shall display the matching annotation text in a tooltip.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Hovering or focusing a marker shows matching annotation text

requirements.text: WHEN user focuses a compact marker via keyboard, the system shall display the matching annotation text.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Hovering or focusing a marker shows matching annotation text

requirements.text: WHEN user clicks or taps a compact marker, the system shall keep the annotation visible until the user dismisses it or selects another marker.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Clicking or tapping a marker keeps annotation visible until dismissed

requirements.text: WHEN user activates the annotation mode toggle, the system shall not modify the source Markdown file.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: The control does not modify the source Markdown file

requirements.text: WHEN a non-Wireloom code fence is rendered, the system shall not show an annotation mode toggle.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Non-Wireloom code fences render exactly as before

requirements.text: WHEN multiple Wireloom blocks appear in one document, each block shall have its own independent annotation mode toggle.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5: Edge Cases - Multiple Wireloom blocks in one document

requirements.text: WHEN user presses Escape while a compact marker annotation is displayed, the system shall dismiss the annotation.
Compact mode shall avoid expanding the rendered SVG canvas dimensions beyond the base mockup dimensions.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Non-Functional - Compact mode avoids expanding rendered SVG canvas

requirements.text: Keyboard and screen reader users shall be able to reach annotation marker controls and read annotation text without a pointer device.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Non-Functional - Keyboard and screen reader access

requirements.text: Malformed Wireloom source shall show the existing inline error behavior regardless of annotation mode.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Non-Functional - Malformed Wireloom source shows existing inline error

requirements.text: Missing or unloadable Wireloom shall fall back to escaped plain code regardless of annotation mode.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Non-Functional - Missing Wireloom falls back to escaped plain code

requirements.text: The annotation mode toggle shall preserve its state when the user switches theme while compact mode is active.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5: Edge Cases - User switches theme while compact mode is active

requirements.text: The annotation mode toggle shall preserve its state when the user opens or closes fullscreen while compact mode is active.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5: Edge Cases - User opens fullscreen while compact mode is active

requirements.text: WHEN multiple annotations target the same element, the system shall display distinct numbered markers at the same target position with unique annotation text for each.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5: Edge Cases - Multiple annotations target the same element

requirements.text: WHEN annotation text exceeds the available tooltip width, the system shall wrap or truncate the text in a readable manner.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5: Edge Cases - Long annotation text exceeds tooltip width

requirements.text: WHEN a Wireloom annotation target cannot be resolved, the system shall skip the marker for that annotation without breaking other markers.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5: Edge Cases - Annotation target cannot be resolved

scenarios.title: Annotation mode toggle is visible on rendered Wireloom block
scenarios.given: a document containing a Wireloom code block is open
scenarios.when: the Wireloom block has finished rendering
scenarios.then: an annotation mode toggle control is visible on the block

scenarios.title: Switching to compact mode replaces callouts with numbered markers
scenarios.given: a rendered Wireloom block is displaying full callout mode
scenarios.when: the user activates the annotation mode toggle to select compact mode
scenarios.then: the always-visible callout boxes are replaced with numbered target markers at the same positions

scenarios.title: Switching back to callout mode restores full callout view
scenarios.given: a rendered Wireloom block is displaying compact mode
scenarios.when: the user activates the annotation mode toggle to select callout mode
scenarios.then: the numbered markers are replaced with always-visible callout boxes

scenarios.title: Hovering a compact marker shows annotation tooltip
scenarios.given: a rendered Wireloom block is displaying compact mode with numbered markers
scenarios.when: the user hovers over a numbered marker
scenarios.then: a tooltip displays the matching annotation text for that marker

scenarios.title: Focusing a compact marker via keyboard shows annotation tooltip
scenarios.given: a rendered Wireloom block is displaying compact mode with numbered markers
scenarios.when: the user tabs to focus a numbered marker
scenarios.then: a tooltip displays the matching annotation text for that marker

scenarios.title: Clicking a compact marker keeps annotation visible until dismissed
scenarios.given: a rendered Wireloom block is displaying compact mode with numbered markers
scenarios.when: the user clicks a numbered marker
scenarios.then: the annotation text stays visible until the user clicks elsewhere, clicks another marker, or presses Escape

scenarios.title: Pressing Escape dismisses a displayed compact annotation
scenarios.given: a compact marker annotation is currently displayed

scenarios.title: Toggling annotation mode does not modify source Markdown
scenarios.given: a document containing a Wireloom code block is open
scenarios.when: the user switches annotation mode between callout and compact
scenarios.then: the source Markdown file content remains unchanged

scenarios.title: Non-Wireloom code fences do not show annotation mode toggle
scenarios.given: a document containing regular code fences (non-wireloom) is open
scenarios.when: the code blocks have finished rendering
scenarios.then: no annotation mode toggle control appears on non-Wireloom code blocks

scenarios.title: Multiple Wireloom blocks have independent annotation mode toggles
scenarios.given: a document containing two Wireloom code blocks is open and both are rendered
scenarios.when: the user switches one block to compact mode
scenarios.then: the other block remains in its current annotation mode and each block has its own toggle

scenarios.title: Full callout mode is the default and preserves current rendering
scenarios.given: a document containing a Wireloom code block with annotations is open
scenarios.when: the Wireloom block has finished rendering
scenarios.then: annotations are displayed as always-visible callout boxes (current default behavior)

obligations.title: Post-process rendered Wireloom SVG to extract annotation positions and support compact mode overlay
Add annotation mode toggle button to Wireloom wrapper DOM, co-located with fullscreen button
Compact mode replaces callout SVG elements with numbered markers positioned at annotation target coordinates
Tooltip/popover shows annotation text on hover, focus, click, and dismisses on Escape or outside click
Toggle operates purely on rendered DOM without modifying source Markdown or Wireloom source
Annotation toggle only appears on .wireloom blocks, not on other code fences or diagram types
Annotation mode state is per-block, stored in-memory on the DOM element via data attribute
Compact mode state persists across theme changes and fullscreen toggle without re-rendering to callout mode
Malformed source and missing Wireloom fallback paths remain unchanged regardless of annotation mode
E2E test file covers all BDD scenarios for annotation toggle
Compact markers are focusable, have ARIA labels, and tooltip content is announced to screen readers; DOM Purify config allows tooltip attributes
Unit tests cover annotation extraction, marker generation, tooltip show/hide, and toggle state management

artifacts.sourceRef: architecture: compact mode marker and tooltip styles
architecture: unit tests for annotation toggle
architecture: modified renderer to support compact mode
architecture: fullscreen must preserve compact mode state
architecture: prose CSS for wireloom block styling
architecture: E2E tests for annotation toggle
architecture: DOM Purify config may need to allow tooltip attributes

testPlans.title: E2E tests for Wireloom annotation view toggle BDD scenarios
Unit tests for Wireloom annotation toggle logic

Integrate annotation toggle into Wireloom renderer and fullscreen
Wire E2E tests and make all BDD scenarios GREEN
Create wireloomAnnotationToggle module with annotation parsing and toggle control
